Title: Feature request: Tests for backend
Body:
### What's the feature 🧐

As discussed in the comments for #305 there are currently no backend tests that actually set up the db, populate it and then test the api. To avoid breaking things accidentally, add tests that verify that the backend is working as expected.

### Extra information and references

I started working on this in my [fork](https://github.com/irishrain/kitchenowl-backend) of the backend - this issue is so you know I'm doing this and avoid duplicate work. I don't have that much free time, so reaching enough coverage for a pull request will take some time. My goal is to have at least tests for a complete cycle of creating a household, creating a user, adding items to the shopping list, adding a recipe, scheduling the recipe, adding the ingredients for the scheduled recipe to the shopping list, and finally clearing the shopping list.

Title: Feature request: Turn KitchenOwl deployment into a monolith
Body:
### What's the feature 🧐

Currently, hosting KitchenOwl yourself requires you to use two containers. One serving the backend and one serving the Web UI.

This approach has some downsides:

- Setup is not as straightforward as it could be, since multiple containers are required
- Contributing features that require changes in backend and frontend must be coordinated across two repositories
- Versions of both containers must stay compatible. There may very well be incompatible combinations of both containers.

I propose the following:

- We unify the repositories by moving all the code of the backend into a "backend" directory in this repository
    - We might loose git history when not doing complex migration logic. This is doable, but though. I saw this work though and I am willing to do this work
    - As an intermediate step, we could use git submodules to include the backend repo into a folder of this repository.
- We let the wsgi serve the WebUI as static file effectively letting `tombursch/kitchenowl` take over the functionality of ``tombursch/kitchenowl-web`
    - To avoid breaking existing users, we could turn the kitchenowl container into a noop in newer versions.

I know this is a big change and in the end, it's your call. But in my opinion, this change would greatly simplify development and deployment.

### Extra information and references

_No response_

Title: Feature request: Multiple Stores per Item
Body:
### What's the feature 🧐

As the title suggests; similar to tags or categories, but as a multi-selection feature, for stores we can (pre)define.

Usecase: We're shopping e.g. food from different supermarkets, and some products are only available (or only "good (value/taste for money") in some of them. We're using  "OI shopping list" (offline app) for years now, and use the store feature there extensively: store, price, unit per store also allows also for simple price comparison; but mainly to filter for only those items I want to buy from the store I'm at. Going through a dedicated shopping list per store wouldn't work though, as e.g. milk is fine from more or less any, whereas the cheese is crap in the one store, but okay two others. You get the idea.

If implemented, an easily accessible filter dropdown in shopping list view would be needed => show me 
- only what I want to buy here 
- AND everything that has no store set 
Logic: If I don't set particular stores for an Item, it's (likely) fine to get this item from anywhere; only after I find out that it's better in some places (or rather: bad in the others), I'll set those "preferred sources". 
This "AND not set"-Logic could eventually be an option to toggle maybe, as users may have a different logic to it. Some are setting every good store on every item always, others do it like I above.

Just wishes ;) ... for starters, we'll get along with descriptions, like putting our abbreviations for the store names in there as a first letter or so.

_No response_

Title: Bug: iOS Safari cursor in recipe description
Body:
### Is this urgent?

No

### What parts are affected

Frontend

### What is the server version

0.4.19

### What is the client version

iOS 17 Safari

### What platform are you using

Web

### What's the problem 🤔

This is kind of difficult to describe, and it's difficult to capture screenshots or videos since it occurs on my phone, so apologies in advance if it's unclear.

For clarity, I want to mention that there are multiple ways to place the cursor in text fields on iOS:
1. Tap the text field in the approximate position of where you want the cursor to be placed.
1. Long-press near the cursor's position _in_ the text field and move your finger around. iOS places a magnifying-glass widget above your finger displaying an enlarged view of the cursor's position under your finger as you move it.
1. Long-press the spacebar on the keyboard and then move your finger around

This report is regarding methods 1 and 2. (Method 3 seems to work okay, based on some quick testing, but I haven't tried it extensively as it's the one I use least.)

# The Bug
When moving the cursor in the "Description" field for a recipe via the website in Safari on iOS, the cursor's actual position will sometimes be placed at a significantly different point in the text than the user's finger. This bug occurs both for long-pressing in the text field (method 2 above) and for tapping (method 1 above).

In some cases, while dragging one's finger to fine-tune the position in method 2, it's actually possible to see a second magnifying-glass widget, sort of double-magnifying the first. I did manage to snag a screenshot of this behavior:

![KitchenOwl (2) - Copy](https://github.com/TomBursch/kitchenowl/assets/23439518/e1dfdf9c-6115-4cc9-b427-ff0cc9303e93)

You can also see a light-gray cursor. That's very approximately where I would expect the cursor to go, but it will remain visible only for a moment, and you can see that it's not actually inside the magnifier, as it would usually be. And you can also see the position where the actual cursor will be dropped (the green cursor).

Apologies, I haven't been able to determine what exactly triggers this bug, or at least makes it bad enough to be noticeable. I think it may be related to the quantity of text - it seems to get worse when there are multiple wrapped lines, and near the end of recipes. On the other hand, a 3-line test recipe with no wrapping is enough to trigger mild versions of it, including the double magnifier.

### Share your logs

_No response_

	at android.os.Binder.shellCommand(Binder.java:950)
	at android.os.Binder.onTransact(Binder.java:834)
	at android.app.admin.IDevicePolicyManager$Stub.onTransact(IDevicePolicyManager.java:6965)
	at android.os.Binder.execTransactInternal(Binder.java:1184)
	at android.os.Binder.execTransact(Binder.java:1143)

```

I searched the error message and it appears that you cannot have accounts on the device for this command to succeed. [Example](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44155652/not-allowed-to-set-the-device-owner-because-there-are-already-several-users-on-t)

I think ideally this process should not require removing all accounts from the device. If that's not possible then there should be a warning in the documentation about this.

Title: [Feature Request] Doomscroll escape to close app as well as exit
Body:
Hi! I am really impressed by this app. Extremely well made.

It would be brilliant if the "doomscroll escape" feature actually closed the app so it doesn't appear in your open apps menu. There are two reasons for this:
1. It would remove the temptation to reopen it when you see it in your open apps.
2. Even if you do reopen the app, shutting it would reset the scroll progress to the top, making it so you can't just pick straight back up where you left off.

Title: Allow pausing for longer durations by long pressing QsTile
Reinforcement Learning
Body:
Many of these services, for example Facebook, use reinforcement learning algorithms (AI) to sustain people returning to them.  Would you ever consider including algorithms of our own, to influence our phone behavior differently?

The usageDirect app has code for getting detailed app usage data on a phone: https://codeberg.org/fynngodau/usageDirect

The openai gym leaderboards have some inspiration regarding reinforcement learning: https://github.com/openai/gym/wiki/Leaderboard

Body: Any plans to add translations? I can help with Polish and Esperanto.

Title: Add option to hide values
Body:
Staring at a "bad" number might make one feel like they're failing at their goals and increase use.

There should be a way to "hide today's use" to prevent this guilt trigger while still keeping track of one's health

Title: Add option to remove outliers from statistics calculations
Body:
As a Daily user, I want to be able to exclude data points that are way too different from my average data point.

We can start with taking the 95 percentile and that will already exclude a lot of outliers.

- Went to a party and consumed more than usual
- Consumed using a different method which changes the price or quantity


- [ ] Checkbox to remember my option (which must be persistent)
- [ ] Take 95 percentile to calculate data on stats

Title: Widgets don't update after I leave the screen
Improving the UI Design of the app.
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Hi there, i feel this type of apps should have a strong influence over its users in order to make changes, and an intuitive & engaging UI can help this app to achieve it. I see there is a room of scope in improving the Design of the app.

Is this project open to accept UI design improvements so that i can contribute on them and code them as well

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to improve the design of the app and code it as well, i am very good in UI Design and i've contributed to open source projects in design improvements before.


**Additional context**
Are the maintainers i mean this project is open to accept UI enhancements? please let me know, i can make some improvement mockups as well and share with you.

Title: 60 and 90 days line color in Stats page are too faint and confusable with the background color
Refactor SettingsView code
Body:
SettingsView is currently 658 lines long, and growing.
Most of the code is repetitive, and can probably be abstracted away.

This should be a priority before adding new configuration options.

Title: Add Detekt to pre-commit hook
Body: Waiting for the checks to run is a slow process. We should have detekt as part of the precommit hooks of this repository

Title: Add option to disable an enabled pause
Body:
"pausing a pause".

To prevent self guilt and worsening of a crisis, one should be able to say "hey Petals, I'm on a crisis right now, suspend the pause for a week"

Title: Add widget for use control to BangleJs
Add "buying date" and "buying amount+monetary value" option
Body:
I would want a way to add a secondary option for the "usage" button that adds a prompt of:

- amount bought,
- date,
- and currency value. 

**Problem solved:**
This way, I can track my consumption better and reduce it properly (ex. I smoke more than usual on the day of buying the stuff, so I can take it into better account if I can know when I bought the stuff. 

**Additional sub-feature** 
I would also like to know when I buy it, how much I buy, and for what price etc for average monthly/daily/yearly buying is possible, using the already existing graphs and data info 'screen'.) 


**Describe alternatives you've considered** none

**Additional context**
Really appreciate the work on the app, it really helped me reduce and track my usage, thank you :)

Title: Add K-Safe suggestion on pause screen
Body: Sometimes self-control can be hard. KSafes are good alternatives to take control out of oneself. That tip can be added to the pause feature

Title: Add option to purchase ad free option
Add rewarded ads options to "disable ads for a week"
Create a guide/gif/pdf on how to use the app displaying the main features and their purposes
Body:
In order to present Petals as a healthy choice and to indeed be a healthy choice, we need to show it to the world. One way of doing that is **showing screenshots**, which is static and not enough to visualize everything. Another way is to show to the person **face-to-face**, but that's not scalable and won't reach much people.

We need something designed to show how the app works, why the features are there and as an invite to get to know it better.
We should be able to show this 'brochure' to

- Potential users
- Researchers that want to understand the project
- Media/press

Title: Add code coverage metrics
Body:
Using kotlinx-kover https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-kover we should try to estimate our current coverage by unit tests.

For example. I know that plenty of the database is covered in unit tests, but UI tests are lacking. How lacking? We don't know.

- [x] Add Kover to the project
- [ ] Create a github action to run it on every push

Title: Use ORM instead of SQLite SQL
Body:
We have quite a lot of code to manage three tables in a SQLite database.
One option would be to use  an ORM like https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx/releases/room


Moreover, all DB interaction is done via ContentProvider.
This complicates the situation even further while it has the advantage that all data can be exposed to other applications.

Plan:
- [ ] OpenTracks accesses SQLite DB directly (without CustomContentProvider)
- [ ] Trim down CustomContentProvider to only provide data for Dashboard API (e.g., for OSMDashboard)
- [ ] Check if ORM/Room helps to get rid of the large amount of data handling code

Title: BLE sensor suitable for Atmospheric Pressure Measurement (0x2A6D) using the Environmental Sensing Service (ESS: 0x181A)
Body:
#1424  continuation

Summary: BLE atmospheric pressure measurement has been introduced in v4.10.1. It works excellently!! :D
Currently, there is no commercially available fully-fledged BLE sensor device supporting Environmental Sensing Service ([0x181A](https://github.com/oesmith/gatt-xml/blob/master/org.bluetooth.service.environmental_sensing.xml)) and the Pressure characteristic ([0x2A6D](https://github.com/oesmith/gatt-xml/blob/master/org.bluetooth.characteristic.pressure.xml)) on the BLE sensor side.
A potential solution could be the development or modification of a BLE sensor, for which the following devices have been considered so far:

Dev boards: Hardware and software development are required:

- [Arduino Nano 33 BLE Sense](https://store.arduino.cc/products/nano-33-ble-sense-rev2)
- [Nicla Sense ME](https://store.arduino.cc/products/nicla-sense-me)

Title: Marker: hold unique reference to TrackPoint.
Body:
OpenTracks only allows to create Markers if a valid location is available (i.e., a TrackPoint with location was stored).
However, in the current implementation the TrackPoint and Marker tables are independent (i.e., Markers also store lat/lng, altitude etc) and they are just indirectly linked together by `time`.
In addition, Markers have the `duration` and `length` (here distance) for being rendered on the charts.

This PR changes this on database level by adding a reference for each Marker to one TrackPoint.

**However:** Both GPX and KML allow to import Markers that are not related to any TrackPoint within a Track.

This implies that people might have Markers that cannot be linked to a TrackPoint - they might not even be close to any TrackPoint (in time as well as location).
Even worse people might have imported such Tracks on purpose to show the Markers with OSMDashboard or just to store all their tracking data in OpenTracks (from other application).

**Question:** Should OpenTracks internal data model reflect how OpenTracks works or rather keep the existing functionality?

PS/ I am not even sure that the functionality was intended this way...

Title: markers imported from a KMZ file which are at the end of the track are shown at the track start
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Import of a KMZ file with markers which are located **after the start** of a track will lead to track charts with the markers only shown **at the start** of the track.

It is interesting that exporting this imported track again leads to a KMZ file which is the same as the imported KMZ file. So the error is somewhere in the OpenTrack internals and does not propagate again to the exports. Searching for the affected markers in the both the imported and again exported KMZ file shows that the markers point to the right places (somewhere far from the start of the track).

Markers are properly shown for tracks recorded directly in the app. This is related only to the imported tracks. Looks like possibly related to comparing floating point numbers precisely (while they differ slightly due to import/export) or maybe some timezone/localTime/GMT related stuff.

I do not care about this bug nor whether it ever fixed. I only wanted it recorded. I detected it by a chance while implementing #1820. That task was marginally related to marker drawing in the charts.

**To Reproduce**
1. Record a track with markers which are not at the very beginning of the track.
2. Export as KMZ.
3. Delete the track.
4. Import the KMZ.
5. Look at a chart of the imported track.
6. All markers will be shown (one over another) at the beginning of the track.

**Technical information**
 - This is likely unrelated to a specific version of Android.  I have seen it in the Android Pixel virtual phone of the current android-studio.
 -  The KMZ file was from my Samsung XCover4s with Android 11.
 - OpenTracks version was a few commits newer than v4.10.1.

Title: do not report units in the announcer
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
My problem is that the announcer talk can be talking for too long and "hiding" too much of a show I'm listening in the background while training.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Do not report the units. They are useless. Sometimes they are too long like e.g. "kilometres per second". They are always the same as set in the settings.

Continue to report the name of the quantity being reported, eg. "Average speed" (or only "Speed" for interval speed average) and the number. The name needs to stay so that we do not need to remember the meaning of reported numbers by their ordinal position. It also allows me to increase concentration to catch the following number.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Unit reporting can be an option in the settings but I think it is not worth it.

Title: Database: introduce Marker.TrackPointId column
Body:
For (unknown) historic reasons, the Marker's table links to the TrackPoint table by storing 
* TrackId
* Latitude
* Longitude
In case there are duplicate TrackPoints (e.g., visiting the same place twice during one track recording), the first TrackPoint is chosen.

I guess, back then this kind of convenient (it is also in line with the exports in KML/GPX).
This is however, not that great as it kind of tricky to maintain.

The goal is to simplify this, by replacing Marker's.latitude/longitude by introducing Markers.TrackPointId.

Affected Modules:
* [ ] Import/Export (KML/GPX)
* [ ] ChartView
* [ ] MarkerList
* [ ] CustomContentProvider / ContentProviderUtils

PS/ as TrackPoint.Id is unique within OpenTracks, Marker.TrackId may be dropped as well.
However, this column may have some performance benefits.

Title: Support for Android 14's Predictive Back Gesture
Body:
OpenTracks uses `onBakPressed()` to intercept back navigation events.

Article that contains the migration path: https://developer.android.com/guide/navigation/custom-back/predictive-back-gesture

https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android/blob/master/docs/foundations/PredictiveBack.md

- [x] opt-in for predictive back (needed for Android 13 only)
- [x] navbar back navigation is (still) working
- [ ] replace all implementations of `onBackPressed()`

NOTE: `onBackPressed()` is to be replaced with `getOnBackInvokedDispatcher()`; however the later is only available for Android 13+. Thus, there is some potential effort for keeping pre-13 and 13+ implementations.

Title: Migrate Bluetooth LE sensor management to CompanionDeviceManager
Body:
With Android 8.0 (API 26), the CompanionDeviceManager was introduced.
This API allows to associate an app with a BLE device and provides features like notify on BLE device availability.

Currently, OpenTracks stores the MAC-Address for the BLE devices and on recording starts, connects to this device.
Best case scenario: OpenTracks would not need to store this information anymore, but use Android's default way.

This allows then to:
* fix #508 (likely fixed within this work)
* implement #1376 

https://developer.android.com/reference/android/companion/CompanionDeviceManager

Title: New Product Creation Defaults to 1 Unit despite Default Amount for Purchase Setting
Body:
I am scanning new items into inventory.

1. Select "Purchase" from the Stock overview card.
2. Scan a new barcode.
3. The create new product screen appears.
4. Add location + select units [grams (g)]
5. Save product >> returned to purchase screen.
6. Amount is auto filled with 1
7. Due date screen appears.
8. Save due date >> transaction begins to auto complete for 1g of product.
9. Press cancel
10. Change amount
11. Save purchase.

Step 6 results because the default consume amount and stock amount get set to 1.
I currently have the setting "Default amount for purchase" set to 0.

I expected it to prompt for the amount, given it's a new barcode/product.
I am making the assumption that it is best practice to store a product that is purchased in grams, but has different bar codes for different box sizes, as a main product, with the barcode linking the purchase amount. However, it is not possible to link the barcode inside the product creation screen. They are greyed out and prompt the user to create the product first.
When the user creates the product, the amount is auto filled, rather than prompting the user for the amount. Grocy ends up associating the new bar code with 1 unit of the default quantity.

Title: Feature Request: Manual Offline mode for shopping list
Body:
Hi,

i've already saw some bugs and feature requests regarding the offline mode for the shopping list (speaking only of marking an item as done and resyncing after going back online). 
So apparently, here in germany most of the grocery stores have really bad mobile coverage indoors and the open wifi is not always that reliable. So often i have the following issue:
My mobile data (EDGE indoors) is doing some ping-pong with the wifi of the grocery store, which leads the app to permanently attempting to sync the shopping list - i guess the app thinks the internet is back an tries to sync. But the result is, that the app is constantly trying to sync the shopping list, showing the spinning circle (of death). 

**Feature Request**: I'd like to have  a small button in the shopping list, that let's me toggle the offline mode for the shopping list. So before i enter a grocery store i switch to the offline mode, and when i leave the store i switch back to online mode and let grocy sync the list.

I know, that i theoretically could switch my phone into flight mode, but i like to be in contact with my spouse (for inquiries :-)). Apperently, messenger like whatsapp and threema seem to work with a bad internet connection (no offense towards grocy android :-)).

What do you think?

Title: Strange quantity when scanning items to fill stock
Body:

When I go shopping and scan the items, on some items, the app automatically adds this item several times instead of just once.

If I do not look exactly on the information after scanning, I sometimes miss, that to many items are added.
So I would expect to always add one item when I scan one item.

Normally if I want to add more items (that also have the same expiry date) I cancel the automatic commit message and increase the item count and then press save.

Normally, even if I bought several items of the same type (e.g. soup) I scan and add them once after another since they have different expiry dates.


So is there some logic behind these behavior, that some items that I scan have an automatic quantity that is more than 1?
Can I somehow change this?


And maybe a related issue:
The same is true if I consume an item.
On some items it happens, that if I scan one item (e.g. take a soup) that automatically 2 or more items are removed from the list.
Also here, if I do not watch carefully, my inventory is already wrong.
Maybe this are the items that were also added as multiples before?

For me this behavior is totally confusing and makes the automatic commit useless, since I do have to always anyway check very carefully, that the amount is correct.

Title: Items out of stock not showing
Body:
When I navigate to the stock overview and (!important) select a specific location, unfortunately I only see items that are not completely out of stock.

But this view would also be great to filter for items that are out of stock for a specific location.
This does not seem to be possible.
I could add them to the shoppinglist, but still then I am not able to filter by location.

It would be great if items that are out of stock (i.e. 0 ) are shown in the stock view not only items that are lower than the desired amount.
Or maybe some filter options to show / include items out of stock.

Title: Grocy Android gives blank page when trying to connect to own server and hangs
Body:
With fresh installed Grocy Android (3.3.2 of 6.9.2023) shows blank screen when trying to connect to own server.  A previously installed version was  de-installed before hand. Connection to demo server works. 
Smartphone is Samsung S9+ with Android 9.

Title: Scanning a unique barcode on "Product" (Consume, Inventory etc. ) still gives selection list
Body:
Whenever I choose something from "Products" on the main page, i.e. "Consume" or "Inventory?" (German: Inventur), tap into the product field and then scan the barcode with the hardware scanner, I always see some different products in a list from which I need to choose the correct one.

This is a bit confusing as the EAN in the field is unique - why does Grocy offer me more products for selection instead of directly picking the right one?

In quick scan mode the same product works without a selection list - but sometimes a "Verbrauch" via the "Products" section is more comfortable, i.e. in case you consume more than one item of the product.

BTW: It would be even more helpful, if the focus by tapping on "Inventur", "Verbrauchen" etc. in the Products section would be directly in the product entry field, so the hardware scanner can be directly used without the need of tapping into the product field first the get the cursor in there.

This is not scanner related, slowly entering the same EAN manually via keyboard also leads to the crash.

Affected EAN are i.e:

Felix catfood, 7613036898591
Sanabelle catfood: 4015598017176

Scanning the same products via standard consume function works, only quick scan is affected.

Title: Subpage occasionally stays empty
Body:
First of all thank you for this really awesome app!

I'm running the app on my FireHD tablets which are normally used for as HomeAssistant display with Fully Kiosk.

Unfortunately, sometimes a sub page stays completely empty. For example, when tapping on the "Quick Scan" button, the page opens completely empty without any elements. Going back and pressing the button again still leads to a fully empty page.

This can be fixed by force-quitting the app and restarting it, but as FullyKiosk monitors the app use that's not so easy ;-)

I also experienced this issue after changing a setting and going back to the main screen, subsequent calls to the settings page also resutling in an empty page with no elements at all.

There is no relation to Fully Kiosk as this also happens when I quit Fully Kiosk and simply use the app on the tablet.

As this does not happen always it's hard to reproduce, are there any steps in case this happens to help with more information?

Unfortunately this has a quite high effect on the Woman Acceptance Factor ;-)

Title: Purchase unit to stock unit conversions don't work with 4.0 server
Body:
Probably related to changes in the server noted in #644: 
> * [x]  The product option "Factor purchase to stock quantity unit" was removed
>   * => Use normal product specific QU conversions instead, if needed
>   * An existing "Factor purchase to stock quantity unit" was automatically migrated to a product specific QU conversion

The below is the result of an attempt to purchase a case of 12 eggs.

![Screenshot_20230924-154637_Trebuchet](https://github.com/patzly/grocy-android/assets/65917913/cdee18b5-4a69-4224-a457-9245977ab0a4)

Note that "1 pack" (the default purchase unit) of eggs in the screenshot above is interpreted as "1 piece" (pieces being the default stock and consumption unit) of eggs. There had formerly been a "Factor purchase to stock quantity unit" conversion of 1 pack to 12 units, which was migrated to a QU conversion when the server was updated. When confirmed, the purchase is indeed entered into stock as a purchase of a single egg, not of 12 eggs.

This particular bug does not occur with the web front-end, as can be seen below.

![image](https://github.com/patzly/grocy-android/assets/65917913/45412184-5064-42ce-a68c-c8965b05a931)

So my suggestion would be to make the start page / home page configurable

Title: [minor] trim barcodes (grocycodes) before processing
Body:
Hi,

for whatever reason, the grocycode-barcodes on my product labels seem to have leading / trailing whitespaces when scanned in grocy-android. Thus, they don't get recognized as grocycodes but get treated as normal (unknown) barcodes, rendering them unfunctional.

<details>
 <summary>Click here for details, probably unrelated to grocy-android.</summary>

---

I am Using DataMatrix barcodes generated in PHP using the [kreativekorp/barcode](https://github.com/kreativekorp/barcode) library and explicitly trim the grocycode before generating the barcode:


```php
$gr_barcode = trim($_POST['grocycode']);
$symbology = "gs1-dmtx-s";
$barcode_options =  [];
$generator = new barcode_generator();
$barcode = $generator->render_image($symbology, $gr_barcode, $barcode_options);
```

Result:
![grafik](https://github.com/patzly/grocy-android/assets/66213768/652e2434-045e-4f9b-b8ca-8b69226c079e)


When I scan this with my external  hardware scanner, the samsung stock camera app or the XZing Barcode Scanner app, everything appears fine.
However, when I scan it in grocy-android or the the [Barcode-to-PC](https://barcodetopc.com/) android app, there seems to be a leading whitespace. 
When generating a DataMatrix code on https://barcode.tec-it.com/de/DataMatrix, everything works as intended.

I suspect this might be an issue regarding the DataMatrix symbology / dialect, either in the PHP library or in the XZing Barcode Scanner API. However, hunting this down would probably take quite some time and require me to replace hundreds of labels, so I'd be really grateful for a quick and painless workaround in grocy-android.

---

</details>

While I'm aware that the root of the problem does not originate with grocy-android, it would make my life a lot easier if leading and trailing whitespaces could be trimmed from the barcode before applying the grocycode regex matcher.

Thank you for the good work you're doing here!

Best regards,
Tobias

Title: Add support for client certificates
Body:
This adds support for providing client certificates (which can be picked from system certificate store or from file system) and for accepting self-signed single server certificates without storing them in system certificate store.
This is realized via two libraries which have minimal impact on the application code and thus are easy to maintain. MemorizingTrustManager only has been forked by me due to a build issue with current gradle version (we should switch back to the original project after my pull request has been merged), InteractiveKeyManager has been developed by me inspired by MemorizingTrustManager.

Title: Only "chore" notification trigger "due-soon" instead
Body:
If you disable the historical "due-soon" notification and only enable "chore" ones you get the "due-soon" but not the "chores"

Context:
I recently started to use grocy and I have some long past due spices or others "not really dangerous" products that are way past their due date. With the last updates (and #661 apparently), I’m now being notified every-day that this items are past their time…
I understand it’s not good on my part but whatever. Now I really wanted to have the chore notif since I’m bad at doing the ones assigned to me regularly and only do them "when I see they needs to be done". Hence this test/bug report.

As a side question, is it possible to still get the "due-soon" but not trigger for "welp, I think he don’t care any-more after a month or so"? Maybe I should just remove their date of items that I don’t feels I want to respect their due-date? Is it possible to add a tag like "don’t notify me, I know this one is overdue and I don’t care"?

I’m still new to grocy, thanks for the app, without it I think I would have already uninstalled the server (entering everything without photographing the barcode seems insane to me!)

Title: [usability] [UI] price setting with automated decimal point
Body:
many modern apps like e.g. Paypal have adapted the userinterfaces to typing values like it is common in POS systems.

5.36€ is achieved by pressing '5'  '3'  '6' instead of '5'  ','  '3'  '6', basically shifting the digits from left to right.

This is obviously a design choice that might have haters and lovers, so its up to the maintainers to decide. I personally however see more and more apps using this input method and think its in general much faster for the amount of values that are typically typed in grocy. In particularly i noticed this today when shopping where i had to add a lot of numbers right after the other

Title: Consume on individual Grocy barcode also decreases other stock entries
Body:
I'm printing individual barcodes for every unit in stock.

Let's imagine I purchased 6 units.
Grocy will print 6 different labels and create 6 stock entries with different stock_ids (e.g. x63079dba2022b)
When I consume one of these barcodes, of course the total quantity decreases.
But then I can scan that same barcode again and the total quantity will decrease again.

I tried the API endpoint (/stock/products/by-barcode/:grocycode/consume) directly and at the second request, I get 
```{`
    "error_message": "No transaction was found by the given transaction id"
}``` and the total stock quantity is not decreased.

So my guess is that the app uses the Grocy barcode to find the product, and then uses the resulting product ID to consume the unit via the API.

I tested this with app v3.3.1 and Grocy v4.0.2

Title: Sleep Tracking Pause Button
Body:
I've discovered something rather important that is missing, a button to pause sleep tracking.
It may seem unnecessary, but on nights when I wake up at 2AM for no reason, I would like to not have 10 hours of sleep throwing off my averages.
If this is implemented, a "sleep interruption" feature could also be added somewhere to represent how much your sleep has been interrupted, and track it.
Thank you.

Title: Add reminders to start/stop tracking of regular sleeps
Body:
# Problem
It happens from time to time that I forget to start or stop the tracking of my daily sleep.

# Desired solution
It would be nice to have the app remind me in the evening and morning to interact accordingly. I imagine a setting where I can simply create multiple time based, recurring reminders that will trigger a notification. Optimally tracking would be started or stopped respectively when the notification (or a button on it) is pressed.

Additionally such reminders could even serve as a call to go to bed.

Title: App "deinstalled" after LineageOS 18.1 -> 19.1 update
Body:
I updated LineageOS on my Moto G7 Power from 18.1 to 19.1 (The MicroG Version from https://download.lineage.microg.org/ )

The "FOSS Warn" app was installed and worked in the past. After the update the App is away... 
This is the only app where this happened. So I guess it's because of FOSS Warn itself?

But some rests were still there, because i can't just install it from F-Droid:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71315/176373118-df41f3dd-1d7c-4698-b1cd-0b0989abf751.png)

I used adb to fix it:
```
~$ adb shell pm list packages | grep "de.n"
package:de.nulide.findmydevice
~$ adb shell pm uninstall de.nucleus.foss_warn
Success
~$ adb shell pm uninstall de.nucleus.foss_warn
Failure [DELETE_FAILED_INTERNAL_ERROR]
```

Title: Formatting of exported CSV
Body:
Currently, the exported file is a CSV with the name of the counter in rows and each instance in a new column. I think it would be better to have the counters in columns and a new row for each new instance. In other words. to "transpose" the spreadsheet. This is easier to get an overview of since we otherwise get a very long list of columns after using the app for a while. It is also the standard for statistics, at least as I've understood it from other apps and academic datasets.

Then there is a second issue of instances on the same column having nothing to do with each other. Basically, each counter is a list and they just happen to be next to each other in a spreadsheet. I'm not sure if this should be changed, but I can imagine for example having names of counters in columns and dates in rows. Something like this:

|            | Counter 1 | Counter 2 |
|------------|-----------|-----------|
| 2021-04-23 | 2         | 5         |
| 2021-04-24 | 0         | 4         |

The problem with this is that we lose time precision for when something occurred. My actual preferred method might be to have the variables "counter name" and "time" in columns and a single occurrence on each row, like this:

| Counter name | Time                |
|--------------|---------------------|
| Counter 1    | 2021-04-23T08:33:24 |
| Counter 2    | 2021-04-23T11:16:54 |
| Counter 2    | 2021-04-23T19:01:12 |
| Counter 1    | 2021-04-24T09:15:32 |
| Counter 2    | 2021-04-24T10:45:44 |

Using UNIX timestamps (or whatever they're called) like present, would of course also be fine.

Title: [FEATURE] Make the skip mark use a separate gesture
Body:
<!-- This template uses the standard feature request template provided by GitHub. 
template copyright belongs to GitHub. -->
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It's not handy for me to have skip marking to be a toggle with completed and not completed (the so called "Change record status" toggle).

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I expect the skip mark ("-") to have an option ("Separate Skip Status") for a separate toggle mechanism. Currently, there is an option to set an exclusive method (single tap/double tap/long tap) for changing a record status and opening a detailed record. I'd see it as a Daily Reminder-like option that, if turned on, would allow the user to select a method for skipping the toggle. Turning it on would automatically set the method to the only remaining one* and make the methods menu active, and not have the skip mark appear when using the "Change Record Status" toggle (instead of toggling between "v, x, -" it would just have "v and x" without the skip mark). When "Separate Skip Status" is turned off, the option would be grayed out, like the reminder time when the daily reminder is turned off.

\* When the Change Record Status = Tap, Open Detailed Record = Double - set the Separate Skip Status's method as Long

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I also thinked about placing Skip inside the Detailed Record menu or having a switch to turn skipping it off. But I think a separate gesture would be a better option.

Title: [FEATURE] Number of times in the cell
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Thank you for the app. I use it more as a todo-list than to build habits. I use today's column as a checklist of what remains to be done. My task has `Daily goal: 3`. I can change Unknown / Not yet evaluated (`?`) to Yes (`v`), No (`x`) o Don't care (`-`). So I can't put an int into a cell, only a tristate. I can see the reasoning behind the tristate, in the context of building a habit (it's like highlighting "Good job!" without putting pressure on the "how much") but I want to track how many times are remaining. To do that, I want to cycle through how many times are done.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The cycle goes:
* `1`
* `2`
* `v` (to mean Completed, 3 in this case)
* `?` (because sometimes I tap on the wrong row and cannot turn back to Not yet evaluated)
* `x` (Intentionally not done)
* restart with `1`, ...

**Describe alternatives you've considered**


**Additional context**

_Originally posted by @g123k in https://github.com/openfoodfacts/smooth-app/issues/4879#issuecomment-1903399559_

Title: In the search bar, if we enter a barcode with spaces, no product will be found
Body:
Hi everyone,

Here is an issue that users may encounter if they input a barcode with spaces: **no product will be found**.
The fix is, as usual, really simple: remove all spaces if there are only digits on the app.
But shouldn't this fix also be applied to the API?

![IMG_0372](https://github.com/openfoodfacts/smooth-app/assets/246838/45d536b3-f2a6-4632-b803-97f085d28cfd)

Title: Send-us an email should be the first item in the Contact screen
Body:
Hi everyone,

I plan to release some videos and other kinds of documents to help people to use the app.
However, access to the email form is tough to find because it's the last item in the contact form.

Clearly, it should be the first one!
![Capture d’écran   2024-01-22 à 08 05 33](https://github.com/openfoodfacts/smooth-app/assets/246838/d06e9065-df3b-40b4-b760-618bce2eb63e)

Title: Make language selection sticky
Body:
### Problem

I use app in French / France.

I'm in a trip in Italy, I enter mainly Italian products. But I have to select Italian on every single photo I upload… (even have to type "Ital" to retrieve it in the list).

### Proposed solution

1. make language selection sticky: 
   * the language remains the same until I change it
   * or the language remains the same for this product, until I change it

2. add a color and/or small icon if the language does not match main language (to prevent errors)
   * and/or add a small message as I edit something not in the main product language

### Additional context

We cannot change main language currently, but it's tracked in https://github.com/openfoodfacts/smooth-app/issues/4771

Also countries is not exposed in mobile app. This is maybe tracked in https://github.com/openfoodfacts/smooth-app/issues/4959

Title: Proposal for improvement of the edition screen
Body:
Hi everyone,

Today we have :

> - Basic details
> - Photos
> - A shortcut to multiple items
> - Ingredients
> - Categories
> - Nutrition facts 
> - Labels
> - Packaging components
> - Recycling instructions
> - Stores
> - Origins
> - Traceability codes
> - Country
> - Additional details

I think the `Country` label is confusing, not only because it's in the singular form, but also because it's challenging to distinguish from Origins. I'd be inclined to rename it Country of Sale.

I also think we should not only rename that item, but also give some structure:

**General information**
- Basic details
- Photos
- Categories
- Labels

**Nutrition**
- Ingredients
- Nutrition facts 

**Environment**
- Origins
- Packaging components
- Recycling instructions

**Product** 
- Traceability codes
- Stores
- Countries 
- Additional details

Providing some structure would really help.
We could even imagine a single screen for the Product section, to avoid 4 screens with only 1 item.

What do you think of this proposal?

Title: Add metadata about local Product, for better refresh flexibility
Body:
### Problem
We store products locally with only one metadata: the timestamp of the latest insertion/update in the local database (`last_update`).
If we want to do smart database refreshes (e.g. new language), that may not be enough.
"Smart" refreshes mean something more refined than "refresh all the products in one shot".

### Proposed solution
The first example that comes to mind is the language. When we download a product, we call the API with a language parameter.
We could store locally the queried language as a metadata, at the same time as we store the product.
With that metadata, we could run queries like "give the local products in history that are not in Italian (new language), most recent first"

Of course, other metadata could be added.
Possibly, additional OFF metadata extracted from the product (like the OFF "last update"). For queries like "give me the local products whose server refresh is more than 2 months old".
Not sure if the country would be as relevant as the language.

### Part of
- #4941

Title: Bump drift from 2.14.1 to 2.15.0
Body:
Bumps [drift](https://github.com/simolus3/drift) from 2.14.1 to 2.15.0.
Release notes

Sourced from drift's releases.

Drift 2.15.0

Drift 2.15 introduces new features improving web support and for sharing database classes against different databases (e.g. sqlite3 and Postgres):

  • Better support for custom SQL types:
    • Custom types are now applied consistently in the query builder API.
    • Add DialectAwareSqlType, a custom type depending on the runtime dialect. This allows writing "polyfill" types that use native date types on Postgres while falling back to a textual representation on sqlite3 for instance.
  • Initial JSONB support: sqlite 3.45 supports a binary JSON format aiming at reducing size and improving performance for JSON operations in SQL. Drift 2.15.0 supports jsonb functions in the query builder through package:drift/extensions/json1.dart. jsonb functions are also supported by sqlparser when analyzing drift files.
  • Runtime improvements:
    • Wasm databases hosted in workers are closed after the last client disconnects.
    • Add enableMigrations parameter to NativeDatabase to disable migrations, a flag useful for existing databases managed with an external schema tool.
  • Add analysis errors for illegal unqualified references to old and new in CREATE TRIGGER statements.

This release fixes a bug in the generator that is potentially breaking: Tables defined in .drift files with a NULL column constraint (e.g. CREATE TABLE users (display_name TEXT NULL, ...)) were not generated correctly - the NULL constraint was absent from the generated schema. This is not a soundness issue since NULL constraints are the default and ignored by sqlite3. However, it means that the actual schema of databases deviates from what drift will now expect, since NULL constraints will be expected from drift 2.15. This can cause issues with the schema validator after upgrading. If you are affected by this problem, the two possible ways to fix this are:

  1. To remove NULL constraints from column declarations in drift files. You don't need a migration for this since they don't affect semantics.
  2. To keep NULL constraints. In this case, you'll have to increment the schema version of your database and use Migrator.alterTable(affectedTable) on all affected tables to make it consistent with the schema with NULL constraints that drift is now expecting.

Also note that this only applies to drift files and not to NOT NULL constraints, which have always been generated correctly. Finally, older snapshots generated with drift_dev schema generate will continue to not report the NULL constraint, meaning that older migration tests won't break due to this change. If you have any questions or concerns about this, please reach out by opening an issue.

  • b6fbfdb sqlparser support for jsonb
  • 8adb3bd Fix sqlite3 download link on windows

  • f84407a Document content-type header for wasm files
  • a683408 Add option to disable migrations
  • b214f2c docs: add one-liner dependency addition instructions in the documentation (#2...

  • 09c6cf0 Close vfs when wasm database is closed
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

app:

  environment:

  - name: SITE_URL
    value: *SITE
  nginx:

  celery:

```

I think there's an existing issue open for what I ran into (https://github.com/wger-project/wger/issues/1406 ?), but when I run with nginx enabled I also can't get static images to load and the css is screwed up. Does nginx provide something specific in this case that I need?


1. Create data (anything, I tried weight entries and nutrition plan) in the webui.
2. Verified that it persists a logout/login from webui.
3. Open mobile app and try to view - there are no results, completely blank as if a fresh install.
4. Attempt to create via mobile app (i see it in webui persistently)

**Expected results:**
I expected persistence to work and that the mobile app matches what I see in the webui

**Actual results:**
See repro steps, but mobile app doesn't match what's in webui, and any changes in mobile app are gone, even from it's own pov, but persist in webui.

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>

Not sure how to get logs from mobile, but here are relevant server logs for mobile client when trying to create/browse a nutrition plan:
```
wger [06/Sep/2023 10:14:31] "GET /api/v2/nutritionplaninfo/6/ HTTP/1.1" 200 285

I *do* see 4 different plans now in the webui, with the different names that i put into the mobile app.

Am I missing an important piece (e.g. my note about nginx above?) or is something broken?

Tim

Title: Rethink data storage
Body:
_This issue is more of a collection of thoughts about data storage to reflect on than a concrete design._

## Use of databases

Sqflite was initially chosen to allow fast access to large amounts of measurements and the possibility to easily relate fields. There came some downsides with it like bad testability and a bit of overhead even without any measurements.

It turns out that sqflite still needs to [read the entire database file](https://github.com/tekartik/sqflite/blob/master/sqflite_common/lib/src/database_file_system_io.dart#L24-L28) and does so from dart code, so the first advantage simply isn't there. Another realization was that SQL is not fit for analyzing blood pressure and the logic needed to be implemented in dart anyway.

Since settings are inherently unique and are stored as JSON objects for better debugability storing them in a database wasn't productive. The use case of multiple profiles was rather fabricated.

## Alternative design

While solutions for this problem can use existing packages, finding good ones is hard and might lead to similar problems like the sqflite solution currently has. Reinventing the wheel is more complex and time-consuming and can lead to the same problems, but these problems will be more actionable.

### Settings

As settings are already JSON serializable and need to be read in their entirety, saving them all in one big file seems more appropriate. 

### Records

A new requirement I would like to introduce for the new measurement model is that it is easy to maintain and expand. It should be possible to introduce attributes to measurements as well as unrelated recorded features like weight and medicines without rewriting large portions of the storage code.

It would be great to track all types of data separately from each other to allow for customization of input fields and use of the app.

Realistically 90% of data requests will be adding a measurement or accessing records of the last month / 30 days. Viewing / Exporting them all is needed rarely.
This could be used to avoid reading data that is not accessed, by splitting measurements across different files. 2 different implementations come to mind:
- Splitting measurements year/month-wise and just loading the most recent file. This has the disadvantage that requesting read permissions for a file on Android takes long (compared to reading a big file) so transition periods between 2 intervals might take longer.
- Having one large file with all measurements and a cache file might solve this problem This is not ideal for adding new measurements, which would need to be added to 2 files (a potential solution could be a format that allows appending, but this still depends on whether the actual OS / Dart implementation reads the file before). Another issue is that there needs to be a last accessed date which will be complicated to store and update.

=> Splitting might not be needed at all, depending on the performance of reading large uncompressed files.

Both approaches have the problem that the file integrity is not checked and once corrupted measurements can be hard to recover. Text entered in notes can mess with separation-characters and parsing (like during export!).

#### Notes

- Taking notes is not very common and will be viewed even less they could be turned into optionally saved fields.
- In practice performance isn't an issue with using an SQL database, it might be fine to still use sql, but through other structures in making expanding easier. Or libraries: [psqlite](https://pub.dev/packages/psqlite)

Title: [FEATURE] [Android] - Adaptive Launcher Icon - VectorDrawable Icon - Themed / Monochrome Launcher Icon
Body:
### What you want:

### Progress:

- [ ] Adaptive Launcher Icon
- [x] VectorDrawable Icon
- [x] Monochrome Icon

I'd like to add these features using a pull request, however, if @NobodyForNothing is willing to provide the original vector file used to create the app's bitmap icon (if one exists), I would greatly appreciate it, as creating vectors from bitmaps can be quite messy. 

### Why you want it:

- Starting with [TargetSDK 26 / Android 8](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/oreo), devices start to use the app's adaptive icon / drawable in order for it to follow user theming and device preferences:
![](https://developer.android.com/static/guide/practices/ui_guidelines/images/adaptive-icon-foreground-background.png)

-  In combination with the [VectorDrawable Class](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/graphics/drawable/VectorDrawable), apps can also use XML vector graphics to render their app icon, which significantly decreases the icon's file size, as it creates the bitmap cache on the fly.

- Starting with [TargetSDK 33 / Android 13](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/13), devices can request an app icon / drawable **vector**'s monochrome version, which can enable the app to render a user-themed / monochromatic version of it's launcher icon:

![](https://developer.android.com/static/guide/practices/ui_guidelines/images/adaptive-icon-monochrome.png)


[Developer Reference Documentation](https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/launch/icon_design_adaptive)

Title: Support Bluetooth LE blood pressure monitor devices
Body:
It would be great to support Bluetooth LE blood pressure monitors via the standard GATT protocol. This would reduce the chances for typos while entering the values as well as enhance comfort. Moreover, such a feature might get users away from commercial apps shipped with devices.
If I understand correctly, the app needs to subscribe to the Blood Pressure GATT service (UUID 0x1810) with the Blood Pressure Measurement characteristic (UUID 0x2A35) and potentially the Date Time characteristic (UUID 0x2A08). The Blood Pressure Measurement characteristic is described in Section 3.31 of the [GATT Specification Supplement](https://www.bluetooth.org/docman/handlers/DownloadDoc.ashx?doc_id=555004).

Title: Can't connect via HTTP
Body:
**Describe the bug**
I can't connect to my Nextcloud server that uses HTTP instead of HTTPS. This server runs on my internal network over a VPN, so HTTPS provides no real benefit and there's no reason for me to go to the trouble of generating a certificate for it and distributing that certificate to all of the devices that connect to it.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Set up a Nextcloud server that uses HTTP
2. Input the URL of the server in Nextcloud Cookbook
3. See error: "Invalid protocol; URL must start with https://"

**Expected behavior**
Nextcloud Cookbook should allow connecting to servers that use HTTP.

**Smartphone:**
 - Device: Pixel 7
 - OS: GrapheneOS 2023103100 / Android 14
 - Nextcloud: 25.0.2

Title: Can't connect to my NC instance - Certificate error
Body:
**Describe the bug**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->

I try to connect the cookbook app (f-droid) to my NC instance where i have few recipes in my cookbook. I have https enabled on my instance. When trying to connect the app, it doesn't log in and instead i have a red error message saying certificate error.

**To Reproduce**

2. Try to connect through the cookbook app.

**Expected behavior**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen. -->

 - OS: Android 13
 - OS instance : Debian 11

Title: Images don't update after editing
Body:
**Describe the bug**
When I edit the "Image URL" field in the recipe edit form and save it, the image doesn't change (even if I clear the "Image URL" field).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Add recipe with the specified valid "Image URL" (of image 1)
2. See image 1 in recipe detail
3. Open edit recipe form
3. Change (or clear) "Image URL" (to URL of valid image 2)
4. See old image 1 in recipe detail

**Expected behavior**
There is image 2 (or no image, resp.) in recipe detail.

**Smartphone:**
<!-- Please complete the following information. -->
 - Device: Samsung Galaxy S22
 - OS: Android 13
 - Version: v0.12.1+27

Title: Better input option(s) for images
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When I want to add image to the recipe, it is quiet problematic. I think this problem has 2 branches, so I divide my text into these.

**Web (or external) images**. When I have an image outside my Nextcloud and want it to add to the recipe, I need to get its URL (complicated for average user), when it's publicly available (web image) or upload it somewhere and copy URL (other images), which is too complicated and time consuming for most users, I think.

**Nextcloud images**. When the image is located on my Nextcloud, I just need to enter path from Nextcloud root. It is better but manual entering isn't very user friendly. Web version of Nextcloud Cookbook has a image selector for this.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'd like to see some option for uploading an external image using this app (just some simple file input, I think there are some many libraries for this purpose), copying its path and inserting it into the input (or better hiding these things and just displaying a preview of the uploaded image). This picker should have a way to select image from Nextcloud files.

Title: Authenticate through Nextcloud files app
Body:

The prefered way of authenticating should be through the Nextcloud files app. As an alternative the user should still be able to authenticate with username and app password.

Title: Add new techniques
Body:
Capturing here a few technique ideas instead of creating a single issue for each one of them.

- [4-7-11 Breathing Technique](https://github.com/mmazzarolo/breathly-app/issues/73)
- [1-4-2 (or 5-20-10) Breathing](https://github.com/mmazzarolo/breathly-app/issues/75)
- [Rectangular (or 5-8-5-8) breathing](https://github.com/mmazzarolo/breathly-app/issues/76)
- ["11 Breathing Practices to Get You Through a Rough Patch"](https://github.com/mmazzarolo/breathly-app/issues/78)

Please comment in this thread instead of creating new issues. 
In order for a technique to be added/evaluated, we need:
- its name
- its description
- its pattern 
- its color, which must work nicely with the other existing colors

Title: Allow units such as servings for food
Body: It seems that a food's carbohydrates can on only be specified in units of 100g. In the US, at least, both commercial and home-cooked recipes usually specify carbohydrates per serving rather than per  weight. While I could convert these foods over to 100g units, in many cases the actual weight of the food is unknown. I often don't travel with a scale to weigh my foods. A good compromise would be to allow us to just specify a per serving carbohydrate

Title: Add option to favourite food database entries and/or to sort by frequency of usage
Body:
In addition to https://github.com/Faltenreich/Diaguard/issues/18 I would like to be able to favourite food database entries and/or have them sorted by usage.
The information about how many times a certain entry has been used is already present.

Since there are a lot of duplicates coming from the different sources, which sometimes also have different or plainly wrong values, I'd like to be able to favourite the entries I deem correct.

Title: Food database entries from external sources use "kJ" as "kcal"
Body:
I noticed that pre-filled entries from outside sources have wrong kJ/kcal values. When compared to the entry on openfoodfacts, it is clear that the app reads the kJ value as kcal, which is then converted back to a very high kJ value.

Example in the app:
"Apfel birnen saft naturtrüb direktsaft 100%" lists 736,4 kJ (176 kcal)

Entry on openfoodfacts lists 176 kj (42 kcal)
https://de.openfoodfacts.org/produkt/4388860589672/apfel-birnen-saft-naturtr%C3%BCb-direktsaft-100-rewe

Title: Undoing a delete of food entries in the log adds empty entry
Body:
Steps to reproduce:

1. Add an entry with X grams of a food item, e.g. 200g of grapes
2. Open the log and swipe the entry to the right
3. When prompted, tap on "undo"

It will create the same entry as before, only stating that it has 0 BE. Closing and re-opening the app/log then shows an empty entry.

Title: Enhance food database search function
Body:
Currently the search function in the food database only searches for the (exact) titel. If I want to search for a brand, it has to be included in the titel to be found. I can also not do a fuzzy search, since it tries to match the precise string.

Example:
I want to find "Milchreis" from "Müller", so I search for "Müller Milchreis" ("Milchreis Müller" does not work).
I get a few results, which have that exact string in the title.
If I only search for "Milchreis" I get dozens of results, some from Müller (more than with the query above), some from other brands.

Title: Custom categories
Body:
Currently Diaguard supports a fixed set of categories that are relevant for most therapies. Since therapies and people differ, it could be very helpful to support categories by allowing the user to add, edit or delete them on their own.

This issue includes a database migration from the current scheme of fixed categories to a more flexible scheme which supports dynamic categories. Futhermore this issue could be seen as a profi-feature and should therefore not distract from the previous handling of categories and measurements.

Title: Statistics with total count of entries per timeframe
Body:
I'd like to see a summary of my insulin injections. Currently I have to scroll through the protocol and manually count them for every day.
An entry to the statistics page would be great.

Body:
I'd like to view daily/weekly/monthly intake of calories, sugar, carbohydrates, etc.
The data is present, it is just not shown in the statistics page.

Title: Export only "sober" bloodsugar levels (Nüchternzucker)
Body:
When exporting and printing my entries for my doctor, I only need the entries right before a meal intake. All other entries are just for me to be able to have a daily curve, but are uninteresting for others.
Is there a way to make the export customizable in such a way that only certain bloodsugar values are exported?

Title: Button to jump to last entry of certain type
Body:
Currently, when I want to look at my last meal or insulin intake, I have to manually scroll and find the last entry myself.
Since there is room under the date on the lefthand side of the screen, I think adding buttons to jump to the last entry of blood sugar, meals, insulin, etc. would greatly enhance the handling of the protocol screen.

Title: Feature: diary: connect line chart with last day
Body: ... because I want to see where the values are trending from.

Title: Feature: second "blood sugar" value
Body:
Be able to enter a value for e.g. flash glukose.
Either as a new type or a second input for blood sugar.
this second value might not be needed for statistics, but for better understand a certain data point.

The other way round it would even be more realistic for CGM users:
- first value flash glukose
- second value (only in some cases checked): blood sugar

Title: Feature: Diary table: distinguish basal and bolus
Body:
In diary view at the table below it would be helpful to split insuline into basal and bolus in separate rows (as they might act differently fast and long)

E.g. some slow basal insuline acting 12 hours is not what you want to see mixed with a fast bolus insuline acting 2 hours.

Title: Add Temperature Tracking
Body: Request: Additional category for tracking temperature.  Am currently using the 'notes' for this purpose.

Title: Improve data handling
Body: It could be better explained how Diaguard handles its data, e.g. regarding the backup. When importing data, users could be asked whether they want to add this new to the existing data or to replace it all. When exporting data, date ranges could be defined. Additionally an in-app option for resetting all user data would be helpful for people who are not too familiar with the Android settings.

Title: Add more limits for fine granular extrema
Body: Currently there are only two limits: hypo- and hyperglycemia. It could be helpful to add more distinct limits in order to differentiate between light and severe hypo-/hyperglycemia.

Title: Recurring reminders
Body: Currently the reminder serves as a short-term helper e.g. for checking the blood sugar shortly after a hypoglycemia. There is no option for a recurring reminder that repeats on a daily or weekly basis. This could be useful e.g. for checking the blood sugar after waking up or before bed.

Title: Add editing for presets
Body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?
<!--
A clear and concise description of what the problem is.
e.g. I'm always frustrated when [...]
-->

Sometimes I want to remove, add, or change a sound for a preset. Since there's no edit/update functionality, first I have to create a new preset and then delete the old one. This seems a little clunky.

### Describe the solution you'd like
<!-- OPTIONAL: A clear and concise description of what you want to happen. -->

My proposal is twofold:

1. Add an edit button for each preset on the Presets page that, when selected, presents a list of the sounds selected for the preset. From here the user may adjust the volume or remove individual sounds.
    - Additionally, there could be an "Add sound" button at the bottom of the list that would simply navigate over to the Library tab.

2. If a preset is actively playing while on the Library tab and sounds are added/adjusted, pressing the save button should present the user with the option of overwriting/updating the preset, or saving as a new preset.

Title: Simplified Chinese (zh-Hans) addition. Traditional Chinese (zh-Hant) relocation.
Body:
Note: Folder name fragments `zh-Hans` and `zh-Hant` are based on iOS localization convention. These folder name fragments still need to be verified to be accurate for Android OS.

**Simplified Chinese (zh-Hans)**

- Adds Simplified Chinese (zh-Hans) translation in `values-zh-Hans`. 

**Traditional Chinese (zh-Hant)**

- Places Traditional Chinese (zh-Hans) translation in `values-zh-Hant`. 
- `values-zh` is redundant now to `values-zh-Hant`.
    - I left `values-zh` in-place to not break the current build as-is.
    - If `values-zh` can be removed in favor of just using a more specific folder name (e.g. `values-zh-Hant`), this would reduced redundancy in the localization workflow.

Title: Local Backup & Backup Format
Body:
Hi, first of all thank you for the great app :).

When I recently tried to backup the data of the app, I noticed that there is no option to just export the file locally, only to share it via mail, messenger etc. I think it would be nice to have the option to just export it to the local storage (and let the user then decide on how they want to actually backup the data - e.g. copy it to a USB drive or directly onto another phone). Personally, I would prefer that over the current way, were you **have** to share (and thus send) the somewhat private data via some more or less secure service. But I think at least the option to save it locally would be nice.

Moreover, after sending it to myself via a secure messenger, I took a look at the generated file. Apart from the fact that I agree with #220 and would appreciate a more specific filename than `dailydozen_backup.json`, I noticed that it actually isn't a JSON file...

The format is one JSON-Object per line, without being in an array and without being separated by a comma, so not valid json:

```
{...}
{...}
{...}
```

Valid JSON would look like this:

```
[
  {...},
  {...},
  {...}
]
```

Is there a reason for this?

Especially when wanting to export the data and evaluate it, it is somewhat cumbersome and unexpected (and possibly even error-prone on the coding-side as some parsing libraries might not like) that it isn't actual JSON.

Title: Per-second countdowns have delay between some counts
Body:
I use the Interval Timer for 6 intervals with 2 minutes exercise, 2 minutes rest. During the audio countdown of 10 seconds to the end of exercise, the app speaks the numbers "10", "9", etc. at roughly 1-second intervals. However, there is a delay before some of the numbers, usually before "8" and "4". 

**To reproduce**:

- Turn on audio for countdowns to the end of an exercise or rest period
- Set up an exercise pattern of 2 minutes exercise and 2 minutes rest, with 6 repetittions
- Start the exercise
- At the end of the exercise interval, listen to the audio countdown of seconds until the end of the interval. Note the time between each number.
- Similarly, at the end of the rest interval, listen to the audio countdown of seconds until the end of the rest interval. Note the time between each number.

**Observed behaviour**:
- Usually, the time between "9" and "8" is longer than the interval between "10" and "9" or between "8" and "7".
- Usually, the time between "5" and "4" is longer than the interval between "6" and "5" or between "4" and "3".
- Sometimes, the delay occurs between other pairs of numbers. The delay is not consistent for every interval countdown in a single exercise program.
- Times between other pairs of numbers is approximately equal.
- I have not measured the time intervals between spoken numbers to see if they exactly equal 1 second.

**Expected behaviour**:
- Equal time interval between each spoken number in a countdown to end of interval. No pauses or hesitation.
- This time interval is 1.0 seconds +/- 0.1 seconds.

**Test environment**: 
- Privacy Friendly Interval Timer app v 1.2
- Google Nexus 6P phone
- Android 8.1.0

Title: App actions
Body:
### Overview

This pull request integrates the Interval Timer with [Google Assistant App Actions](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/overview). It allows the user to use voice commands to control the application. While this is a feature proposal rather than a bugfix, we hope that it will still attract your attention - it feels natural for an application that is mostly used during physical activities to be used without directly interacting with the screen.

The following voice commands are supported: starting, stopping, pausing, and resuming the exercise. Together, they allow the user to fully control the flow of their exercise using only their voice.

You can see the demo video [here](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1u45jtXWqlXso4y267-iGJ9uRLmxfA2cu/view?usp=sharing).

This Assistant integration fully respects the privacy principles of the Interval Timer: No information is passed from the application to the Assistant. Only if the user actively and knowingly interacts with the Google Assistant, then the Assistant is aware of user actions. Thus, it is transparent for the user whether they share data with the Assistant.

### Authors
This pull request was created as a part of Google STEP Internship project.

### Additional information

**App Actions**

[App Actions](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/overview) are used to integrate third-party applications that want to use voice commands with Google Assistant.

Implementing App Actions for the application means that the application is able to receive implicit intents (called Built-In Intents) sent out by Google Assistant. Assistant is able to parse the user queries naturally - for example, “Begin my run” is correctly interpreted as START_EXERCISE intent.

The feature is relatively new, and available on Android 5 (API level 21) and higher. 

The relevant Built-in Intents, which were used in this pull request specifically, are [START_EXERCISE](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/reference/built-in-intents/health-and-fitness/start-exercise), [STOP_EXERCISE](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/reference/built-in-intents/health-and-fitness/stop-exercise), [PAUSE_EXERCISE](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/reference/built-in-intents/health-and-fitness/pause-exercise), and [RESUME_EXERCISE](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/reference/built-in-intents/health-and-fitness/resume-exercise).

To test App Actions behaviour before deployment, the [App Actions Test Tool](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/test-tool) can be used. It imitates the Google Assistant behaviour, sending out an intent which can then be received by the application. Since the Assistant needs to know which types of queries are supported by the application, using the Test Tool still requires an internal release to be uploaded in Console.

**Deployment**

The full instruction for deployment of an app that uses App Actions is available [here](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/get-started#request-review).

The process of publishing is straightforward, but the deployment needs to be reviewed by Google Play. For this, please fill out [the deployment request form](https://developers.google.com/assistant/app/review). It only asks about general information about your application and the App Actions implemented, and takes only 1-2 minutes to fill out.

---

Please do not hesitate to contact us back if you have any questions regarding the pull request or the deployment process; we will be happy to address them.

Title: Improving acoustic signal for end of intervals
Body:
At the moment it seems to me that you do not get an acoustic signal when the interval (workout or rest) is over. You can either enable the countdown audio announcements or have a timer signal for each second. 

For me thereby the most basic signal is missing (or I do not understand how to enable it): a simple signal (loud beep that is also audible if you listen to music on the mobile plus an optional vibrate) whenever the workout time and the rest time intervals are over. 

It feels strange if you train with enabled countdown announcements and there is a "5, 4, 3, 2, 1," and then no acoustic feedback when the interval is really over. This makes it hard to use the timer without constantly watching at the screen. (And just using the countdown and counting yourself to "0" is inconvenient when you use short Tabata intervals, e.g. a 0:20 workout 0:10 rest interval as you end up with constant countdowns and no option to just get a beep once the time is over.)

Would it be possible to add such a acoustic signal and as a nice to have the option to have a vibration as well? 

PS: Thank you very much for the, in my view, big improvement to define all levels or workout and rest intervals + sets. This is a huge improvement to the old restrictions, especially for the sets.

Title: Add Habits to Weblate to help and promote localization
Body:
Have you ever thought about using a translation platform to handle app translations?

I can warmly recommend [Weblate](https://hosted.weblate.org). It is provided free of charge for FLOSS projects.

Weblate itself is FLOSS too. It is a home for many well known open source projects like F-Droid, OsmAnd, KOReader, DAVx5, Tasks.org, AndrOBD, OpenWRT, KiCad EDA, LibreCAD, Minetest, Orbot, Freedombox, several Debian related projects, and many, many others.

After the initial project setup it is a matter of a `push` to send new strings to translators and a `pull` to get the latest translated content back. That's the manual mode…

<details>
The project has to apply (https://hosted.weblate.org/hosting/) for the free service, providing some data proving FOSS license and such but is simple and for about a week (most of the time few days) the application (let's hope for) is approved. While the application is being reviewed the project is accessible to translators with a warning at the top of the page explaining that the project is not approved yet.
</details>

I'm asking because I want to continue translating the app and this will greatly improve my and other translators' workflow and will ease onboarding new fellow contributors. Plus, Weblate handles [fastlane](https://docs.fastlane.tools/getting-started/android/setup/) meta too.
