Title: RawGit is shutting down, replace it with jsDelivr
Body:
[RawGit](https://rawgit.com/) is shutting down. This PR replaces all RawGit links with the recommended alternative - [jsDelivr](https://www.jsdelivr.com/). jsDelivr is a free and fast open source CDN hosting all files from GitHub and npm.

*This is an automated PR which is meant to make the migration process as easy as possible. If something doesn't look right or you have any questions, feel free to ping MartinKolarik or jimaek in this PR. You may also use our [online migration tool](https://www.jsdelivr.com/rawgit) to convert other RawGit links to jsDelivr links.*

Title: Doesn't remember password on Lollipop
Body: Android Lollipop WebView doesn't allow saving of passwords in WebViews. Entering passwords every time I open the app is annoying, but it may be something unavoidable. If future versions of Android wont support this, then perhaps I'll need to look at web-scraping, or looking upstream to support an API that can be used.

Title: Make iOS version
Body:
A lot of St John members have iPhones. Thus, this should also be supported on iPhones. 

I've had a look into tools to cross-compile for both platforms (e.g. PhoneGap and Appcelerator), but neither is suitable. PhoneGap doesn't allow nested WebViews, and Appcelerator is really hard to build without signing up their site to get the dev tools (which means it wont build for F-Droid).

Thus, it looks like a native client is the option. I currently have no knowledge of the iOS development ecosystem (e.g. XCode/Cocoa/Swift), but am willing to learn. The trick is to first get a Mac which runs all this crap.

Title: Allow for more O2 sensors
Body:
The Divesoft Liberty has four O2 sensors. So far, we had a hard coded limit of three sensors and crashed with a failed assert when we encoutered more than three. This allows for up to
MAX_O2_SENSORS which is currently 6. The voting logic is adapted accordingly.

In some of the import/export functions the value is still hard coded to 6 thanks to explicit field names.

In particular, this removes an assert that triggered when more than four sensors were detected.

Body: ### Describe the issue: - [x] Bug - [ ] Change request - [ ] New feature request - [ ] Discussion request ### Issue long description: When I am dealing with another Shearwater Cloud database import failed issue #3958 I think that export logs as UDDF (Universal Dive Data Format) files from Shearwater Cloud and import them to Subsurface might be a workaround However, when I am checking the imported logs, I notice that **the `Gas type` is incorrect (always be Air) and all the `temperature` samples are lost** By exporting the imported logs as UDDF, I notice that only the first part of the `` is used, which is the gas for CC instead of OC (see Current Behavior part), and the `` in each sample is lost * **To be updated** 1. Not all the logs lost the temperature data, I will try to figure it out the difference and why 2. The CCR mode may have similar issue, I will check it out ### Operating system: macOS Sonoma 14.1.1 (Apple M1) Shearwater Cloud: 2.10.2 Dive Computer: Petrel 3 (standalone) with Firmware v97, Log version v14, DB version v11 ### Subsurface version: v5.0.10 (official release, cca21cf) ### Steps to reproduce: 1. Select one (or many) log and export `As UDDF` using Shearwater Cloud (the gas used was `EAN35`) 3. Import the log into Subsurface 4. The Gas becomes `Air` instead of `EAN35`, and all the temperature samples are lost ### Current behavior: 1. All the `` fields are lost in `` within `` (except the first one) + ### Expected behavior: Should be imported correctly ### Additional information: Remove the `.txt` extension (just in order to upload to GitHub) 1. Title: UI: hide first gas change
Body:
<!-- Lines like this one are comments and will not be shown in the final output. -->
<!-- Make sure that you have read the "Contributing" section of the README and also the notes in CodingStyle. -->
<!-- If you are a collaborator, please add labels and assign other collaborators for a review. -->

Adds an option to hide the first gaschange event, defaulting to `true` to keep the current behaviour.

### Describe the pull request:
<!-- Replace [ ] with [x] to select options. -->
- [ ] Bug fix
- [X] Functional change
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Code cleanup
- [ ] Build system change
- [ ] Documentation change
- [ ] Language translation

### Pull request long description:
<!-- Describe your pull request in detail. -->

The first gaschange event is hidden if it occurs at the beginning of the dive log as it is considered unimportant.

However in certain scenarios Subsurface imports "phantom cylinders" which did not actually exist on a dive. If such a "phantom cylinder" is attached to the first gaschange event, there is no way for the user to delete such a cylinder, since there is no way to edit or delete the first gaschange event using the UI.

This change adds an option to show the first gaschange event. Or rather, to hide it, and it defaults to true. This means that by default, the program behaves as it always did, but now users have an option to toggle hiding this event. If it s shown, the user can edit or delete the event, and thus edit or delete the cylinder to which the event is attached.

### Changes made:
<!-- Enumerate the changes with 1), 2), 3) etc. -->
<!-- Ensure the test cases are updated if needed. -->

1) Add a new preference option to hide the first gaschange event (defaulting to true). This was added in the `Display -> Dive profile depth grid` section, which has been renamed to `Dive profile options`. Both the existing `Depth line intervals` and the new `Hide first Gas Change` options affect the same part of the Subsurface UI, so it was natural to group them together.

2) Check the new preference option and only hide the first gas change events if this option is set to `true` (the default).

3) Unrelated, but during development of this feature it was discovered that the `map_short_names` preference setting was not being explicitly initialized to `false`. Fixed.

### Related issues:
<!-- Reference issues with #<issue-num>. -->
<!-- Write "Fixes #<issue-num" to notify Github that this PR fixes an issue. -->

While this change does not fix #3935, it does allow the user to mitigate it by editing the dive data of affected dives using the UI.

### Additional information:
<!-- Include sample dive log or other relevant information to allow testing the change where feasible. -->



### Release note:
<!-- Describe if this change needs a release note present in CHANGELOG.md. -->
<!-- Also, please make sure to add the release note on top of the file CHANGELOG.md. -->

Yes.

"desktop: add `hide first gas change` preference option."


### Documentation change:
<!-- If this PR makes changes to user functionality, then the documentation has to be updated too. -->
<!-- Please, briefly outline here what has changed in terms of the user experience (UX). -->
<!-- If UX changes have been made, a maintainer should apply the 'needs-documentation-change' label. -->

New option, `Hide first Gas Change` in the `Preferences -> Display` tab.
This option is checked by default, which means that the first gas change event is hidden from view. This reflects the behaviour of Subsurface prior to release XXX. For most users and most of the time this event is just clutter, however in certain circumstances it may be useful for the user to enable viewing these events in order to be able to edit them.

Title: Media not shown on dive profile if no "real dive computer" was used
Body:
<!-- Lines like this one are comments and will not be shown in the final output. -->
<!-- If you are a collaborator, please add labels and assign other collaborators for a review. -->

### Describe the issue:
<!-- Replace [ ] with [x] to select options. -->
- [x] Bug
- [ ] Change request
- [ ] New feature request
- [ ] Discussion request

### Issue long description:
I just noticed that in my newer dives, media (photo thumbnails) are not shown on the dive profile despite of them being available and mapped correctly. Adding a new dive manually and importing the media from a local disk, they are shown on the graph immediately after importing them – but as soon as I switch to another dive and return they are gone from the profile, while remaining in the media tab (e.g. similar to what is described in #2833 – with the difference that for me they never show up again in the profile even if I toggle all the buttons).

Scrolling through my dives to older ones I saw media are shown for those (dives 92 and before with the attached XML). Looking for the difference I noticed they are shown for all dives where data from a "real dive computer" exist (I had one once but it broke) – but for none of the others.

### Operating system:
* OS: Linux Mint 20.3
* desktop PC (no DC anymore, data entered manually, never imported from a DC before as mine was broken before I started with Subsurface)
* cannot test on another OS as I run only Linux

### Subsurface version:
* Subsurface 5.0.10 (latest AppImage)
* not tested on another version (if you let me know which I shall test, I can take a look)
* official release (AppImage downloaded [from the official place](https://subsurface.github.io/download/))

### Steps to reproduce:
1. manually add a dive and fill in the details
2. go to the media tab, right click, selecting files from local disk which fit into the time frame of the dive (my ActionCam has the correct time set)
3. see their thumbs showing up in the tab and as well on the profile graph
4. switch to any other dive previously imported/added
5. switch back to the new dive: media still in the tab, but no longer in the profile graph.

### Current behavior:
Media disappear from the profile graph after having shown up correctly mapped at the initial import.

### Expected behavior:
Media showing up in the profile graph every time the corresponding dive record is selected

### Additional information:
Here's my XML file (extracted around 1 MB) zipped: [xml.zip](https://github.com/subsurface/subsurface/files/13034607/xml.zip). I can reproduce the issue every single time I open it with Subsurface.

Body:
<!-- Lines like this one are comments and will not be shown in the final output. -->
<!-- If you are a collaborator, please add labels and assign other collaborators for a review. -->

Guys, thanks for this awesome tool!

### Describe the issue:
<!-- Replace [ ] with [x] to select options. -->
- [x] Bug
- [ ] Change request
- [ ] New feature request
- [ ] Discussion request

When adding a dive manually that uses more than 1 cylinder, the gas change event is not saved to the XML. It is shown in the editor for the time of editing, but when de-selecting and re-selecting the dive, they dissappear from the profile. Having a look at the XML reveals, that they are not saved to file.

### Issue long description:
<!-- Describe the issue in detail. -->

- When adding log entries via "Add dive", the gas change events are not applied in the profile viewer and not saved to XML
- When adding the same dive via "Plan dive" and planning it for the past, the gas change events are shown in the profile viewer and saved to XML
- A dive that was created manually via "Add dive" (not planned) can be edited via "Edit dive in planner" and if the gas change event is added there, it is saved to the XML

--> Therefore, the bug only persists when adding multi-gas dives via "Add dive"

### Operating system:
<!-- What OS are you running, including OS version and the language that you are running in -->
<!-- What device are you using? -->
<!-- Only answer this question if you have tried: Does the same happen on another OS? -->

- Windows 10 Pro (10.0.19045 Build 19045)
- AMD Ryzen 7 7700X

### Subsurface version:

Title: Convert displayed depths depending on water density?
Body:
<!-- Lines like this one are comments and will not be shown in the final output. -->
<!-- If you are a collaborator, please add labels and assign other collaborators for a review. -->

### Describe the issue:
<!-- Replace [ ] with [x] to select options. -->
- [ ] Bug
- [ ] Change request
- [X] New feature request
- [ ] Discussion request

### Issue long description:
<!-- Describe the issue in detail. -->
I recently went diving in freshwater and my computer assumes I'm diving in saltwater so it logs all my depths a little bit shallower than my actual depth... 97fsw instead of 100ffw

I suppose I could edit the database and multiply all my depths by 1.030, but it'd be nice to have subsurface do that (perhaps just in the display) if I set salinity to freshwater. Having that control for extra dense water would be good too, just in case I ever get the chance to dive the dead sea.

### Operating system:
<!-- What OS are you running, including OS version and the language that you are running in -->
<!-- What device are you using? -->
<!-- Only answer this question if you have tried: Does the same happen on another OS? -->

Ubuntu 22.04 x86_64 en_US
Android 13 en_US

System 76 Lemur 10 Pro
Google Pixel 6

### Subsurface version:
<!-- What version of Subsurface are you running? -->
<!-- Does the same happen on another Subsurface version? -->
<!-- Are you using official release, test build, or compiled yourself? -->
<!-- Provide Git hash if your are building Subsurface yourself. -->

Subsurface 5.0.10
Subsurface-mobile Version: 3.4.7 (5.0.9.35)

Official Builds:
https://subsurface-divelog.org/downloads/Subsurface-5.0.10-x86_64.AppImage
https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=org.subsurfacedivelog.mobile&hl=en_US&gl=US

### Steps to reproduce:
<!-- Provide reproduction steps separated with new lines - 1), 2), 3)... -->
1. Dive to a known depth in fresh water with a computer that doesn't have SW/FW setting.
2. import dive into subsurface.
3. observe that the dive is logged in equivalent saltwater depth
4. change water type to freshwater

### Current behavior:
<!-- What is the current behavior? -->
Dives are displayed exactly as the computer recorded them. Not a bad thing.

Using the water type/density selector does not have any effect on the displayed depths.

### ~Expected~ Desired behavior:
<!-- What is the expected behavior? -->
If the dive computer doesn't specify salinity, assume recordings are based on salt water. Rescale the depth with a conversion factor based on the selected water type; 1.030 for freshwater, or something else as appropriate.

Let's say some point in my dive was 20m (assuming 1.030g/l salt water). I select fresh water, display now says that point was 20.6m. I select some custom density (1.240 for the dead sea), and the display rescales that point to 16.6m.

### Additional information:
<!-- If a simple dive log file can reproduce the issue, please attach that to the report. -->
<!-- With dive computer download issues consider adding Subsurface log file and Subsurface dumpfile to the report. -->

Title: Save pedometer values when memory is low
Body:
When Android experiences memory pressure it will let applications know. After this notification, Android may choose to kill applications to free up memory.

If this app receives a low memory callback, save current pedometer values so that progress is not lost. When the app is relaunched, either automatically when memory pressure is relieved or when it is manually opened, the most recent values will be loaded.
